query: US spot Bitcoin ETF net flows late June 2026 and early July 2026, weekly trend from Farside Investors and SoSoValue. Are net outflows continuing into the week of June 29-July 3 2026? What is the Bitcoin price trend in late June 2026?
answer:
I've completed the research. Here's a summary of the key findings:

## Answer to the Research Question

**Did net outflows continue into the week of June 29–July 3, 2026?** — Partially. Outflows continued for the **first three sessions** (Jun 29: −$244.5M, Jun 30: −$247.6M, Jul 1: −$284.9M), then **reversed to net inflows** on Jul 2–3, ending the streak.

**The key event:** A ~10-consecutive-day outflow streak totaling **~$2.73 billion** ended on **July 2, 2026** with a **+$221.72 million inflow** — the largest single-day intake in ~2 months (per SoSoValue/CoinDesk). Fidelity's FBTC led with ~$166M, while BlackRock's IBIT still saw a $40.43M outflow.

**Weekly/monthly trend:** June 2026 was the **worst month on record** for US spot Bitcoin ETF outflows (~**$4.06 billion**, SoSoValue), beating the prior record of $3.56B (Feb 2025). Late June had brutal daily outflows (Jun 25 peak ~−$696–707M).

**Bitcoin price (late June):** A sharp **downtrend** — from ~$62,651 (Jun 24) to below $60,000 for the first time since late 2024, bottoming at **21-month lows under $58,000** (~$58,503 on Jun 30). It then **rebounded** to ~$61,700 by July 3 and low-$63,000s by July 7 (up ~6% on the week).

**Source note:** There's a minor date-labeling discrepancy — Farside/bitbo split the July rebound across Jul 2 (+$32.3M) and Jul 3 (+$170.6M), while SoSoValue-based reporting attributes the full +$221.7M to a single session (T+1 settlement difference). I flagged this in the report.

Full prose synthesis, structured data points, and all sources have been recorded via the StructuredOutput tool.

key_facts: Net ETF outflows continued only for the first three sessions of the week of June 29–July 3, 2026 (Jun 29: −$244.5M, Jun 30: −$247.6M, Jul 1: −$284.9M), then reversed to inflows.
A ~10-day outflow streak totaling ~$2.73 billion ended on July 2, 2026 with a +$221.72 million single-day inflow — the largest daily intake in ~2 months (per SoSoValue/CoinDesk).
June 2026 was the worst month on record for US spot Bitcoin ETF outflows: ~$4.06 billion (SoSoValue), beating the prior record of $3.56B (Feb 2025).
June's largest single-day outflow was ~$696–707M (Jun 25 shown as −$707.2M on bitbo/Farside).
Fidelity FBTC led the July 2 rebound with ~$166M inflows while BlackRock IBIT still recorded a $40.43M outflow that day.
Total US spot BTC ETF AUM fell to ~$72.82 billion by end of June 2026.
Bitcoin touched 21-month lows under $58,000 in late June (≈$58,503 on June 30), after trading ~$62,651 on June 24.
Bitcoin rebounded to ~$61,500–$61,700 by July 3 and low-$63,000s by July 7 (up ~6% on the week).
YTD net ETF outflows cited at ~$4.6B (late June, Token Metrics) to ~$5.4B (July 3, CoinDesk).
Whales accumulated ~270,000 BTC (~$16.7B) near $59K over two weeks even as ETFs saw record redemptions.

data_points.metric: Bitcoin ETF single-day inflow ending 10-day streak (SoSoValue)
